history.qwen_rationale: Manually adding test dependencies with hardcoded versions often conflicts with parent POM dependency management or fails to resolve correctly. Replacing the manual AddDependency calls with UpgradeSpringBoot_3_2 ensures that Spring Boot and all its managed dependencies (including test starters) are upgraded consistently and resolved correctly before the Java version bump, aligning with the compatibility matrix.

history.qwen_observation: Test compilation failed due to missing/conflicting JUnit 5 and Spring Boot test classes, likely caused by prematurely injecting Spring Boot 3.2.0 test dependencies into a project still running on Spring Boot 2.x/javax.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing explicit test dependency additions and strictly skipping test compilation in the first step avoids classpath conflicts and allows OpenRewrite to analyze the main source tree. We then safely walk Spring Boot up through 2.7 -> 3.0 -> 3.2 to handle the javax-to-jakarta migration incrementally, followed by plugin/Lombok updates and the Java 21 build bump.
